title: Kisan Knowledge — starter pack
source: Government of India open data (data.gov.in, Agmarknet) + Ministry of Agriculture publications. Snapshot bundled with Saarthi v1.0.24.

entries.topic: PM-KISAN Samman Nidhi — direct cash benefit
entries.content: The Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN) is a Central Sector Scheme launched in February 2019 that gives every eligible landholding farmer family ₹6,000 per year in three equal instalments of ₹2,000, transferred directly to the Aadhaar-seeded bank account every four months. Land records are verified by the State / UT government; income-tax-payer families, institutional landholders and former / current constitutional post-holders are excluded. Beneficiaries register through the PM-KISAN portal or a Common Service Centre (CSC); status of payments can be checked at pmkisan.gov.in with the Aadhaar or registered mobile number.

entries.topic: Kisan Credit Card (KCC) — short-term crop loan
entries.content: The Kisan Credit Card scheme provides short-term credit for cultivation, post-harvest expenses, household consumption, working capital for farm-asset maintenance and investment credit for allied activities like dairy, fisheries and animal husbandry. The interest rate is 7% per annum with a 3% prompt-repayment incentive that brings the effective rate down to 4% on loans up to ₹3 lakh repaid on time. Both crop loans (under interest subvention) and term loans for farm investment are covered. Any farmer — owner-cultivator, tenant farmer, oral lessee, sharecropper or SHG member — can apply through the nearest commercial bank, RRB or cooperative bank.

entries.topic: Minimum Support Price (MSP) — key Kharif and Rabi crops
entries.content: MSP is the price at which the Government procures crops directly from farmers via FCI, NAFED and state agencies. For Rabi marketing season 2024-25 the Cabinet Committee on Economic Affairs fixed MSP for wheat at ₹2,275/quintal, mustard at ₹5,650/quintal and gram at ₹5,440/quintal. For Kharif marketing season 2024-25 paddy (common) is ₹2,300/quintal, paddy (Grade A) ₹2,320/quintal, jowar (hybrid) ₹3,371/quintal, bajra ₹2,625/quintal, tur (arhar) ₹7,550/quintal, moong ₹8,682/quintal, urad ₹7,400/quintal, groundnut ₹6,783/quintal, soybean (yellow) ₹4,892/quintal and cotton (medium staple) ₹7,121/quintal. CACP recommends MSPs by computing A2+FL cost (paid-out cost plus imputed family labour) and adds a margin of at least 50%.

entries.topic: Soil Health Card — testing and balanced fertiliser advice
entries.content: Under the Soil Health Card scheme every farm is sampled once every three years and the soil is tested for 12 parameters: pH, electrical conductivity, organic carbon, primary nutrients (N, P, K), secondary nutrients (S), and micronutrients (Zn, Fe, Cu, Mn, B). The card provides crop-wise fertiliser recommendations along with adjustments needed to maintain soil health, and is delivered free to the farmer through the State Agriculture Department. Farmers can collect samples themselves using the prescribed procedure and submit them to the nearest Soil Testing Laboratory.

entries.topic: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY) — crop insurance
entries.content: PMFBY is the flagship crop insurance scheme covering pre-sowing to post-harvest losses caused by non-preventable natural risks. Farmer premium is 2% of sum insured for Kharif food and oilseed crops, 1.5% for Rabi crops, and 5% for annual commercial / horticultural crops; the balance is paid by the Centre and the State in equal shares (the Centre shoulders the full subsidy in north-eastern states). Enrolment is voluntary for all farmers since 2020. Claim disbursement uses Crop Cutting Experiments (CCE) for yield-based losses and remote-sensing add-ons for prevented-sowing and post-harvest losses. Farmers apply through banks, CSCs or the National Crop Insurance Portal.

entries.topic: Drip and sprinkler irrigation — PMKSY Per Drop More Crop
entries.content: Under the Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchai Yojana (PMKSY) — Per Drop More Crop component, central assistance for micro-irrigation is provided at 55% of system cost for small and marginal farmers and 45% for other farmers; many states top this up further. Drip irrigation typically cuts water use by 30-70% versus flood irrigation, increases yields of orchard crops and vegetables by 20-90% and improves nutrient-use efficiency through fertigation. Applications go through the State Horticulture / Agriculture Mission or the PMKSY portal. The Micro Irrigation Fund (MIF) at NABARD provides loans to states for additional subsidies and dedicated micro-irrigation infrastructure.

entries.topic: Wheat sowing calendar and recommended varieties (North India)
entries.content: In the Indo-Gangetic plains the optimum wheat sowing window is 1 November to 25 November; late sowing after 15 December reduces yield by 25-40 kg/ha per day of delay. Recommended varieties for timely sowing under irrigated conditions: HD-3226, HD-2967, PBW-725, DBW-187 (Karan Vandana), DBW-303 (Karan Vaishnavi). For late sowing: HD-3059, HD-3271, WH-1124, DBW-173. Seed rate is 100 kg/ha (timely) to 125 kg/ha (late). Recommended NPK is 150:60:40 kg/ha; the full P and K plus one-third N is applied as basal, with the remaining N split between first irrigation (CRI stage, ~21 DAS) and tillering. Two to four irrigations are needed depending on rainfall and soil.

Healthy paddy nurseries need 600-800 g of seed per 100 m² nursery bed for raising seedlings to transplant 1 hectare. Seed should be treated with Carbendazim @ 2 g/kg seed for blast and Bavistin / hot water (52°C for 30 min) for nematodes before sowing. Nursery is ready for transplanting at 21-25 days for short-duration varieties and 28-35 days for medium/long-duration varieties. Transplant 2-3 seedlings per hill at 20 × 15 cm spacing in fertile soils, 20 × 10 cm in light soils. Avoid transplanting older than 35-40 day seedlings — yield drops sharply. Maintain 5 cm standing water during tillering and panicle initiation.

entries.topic: Fall armyworm (Spodoptera frugiperda) in maize — IPM advisory
entries.content: Fall armyworm has been a major pest of maize in India since 2018. Scout weekly from V3 stage by inspecting 10 plants in each of 5 spots per acre; treat when egg masses or 1-3 larvae per plant are found on >5% of plants. Cultural: deep summer ploughing, balanced fertilisation (avoid excess N), inter-cropping with pulses, light traps at 1/acre, and pheromone traps at 4-5/acre for monitoring. Mechanical: hand-picking and crushing egg masses and early-instar larvae; whorl application of dry sand + lime (9:1) for early infestation. Biological: Trichogramma chilonis @ 50,000/acre at egg stage; Telenomus remus @ 200,000/ha. Chemical (when ETL crossed): spinetoram 11.7 SC @ 0.5 ml/L or chlorantraniliprole 18.5 SC @ 0.4 ml/L or emamectin benzoate 5 SG @ 0.4 g/L — direct spray into the whorl in early morning or late evening. Rotate modes of action across sprays to delay resistance.

entries.topic: e-NAM (National Agriculture Market) — selling at remunerative prices
entries.content: e-NAM is the pan-India electronic trading portal that networks existing APMC mandis to create a unified national market. Farmers can list their produce online after delivering it to any e-NAM mandi; quality is assayed and bidding takes place electronically. Major benefits: better price discovery via wider buyer participation, transparent weighment, electronic payment within T+1 to the farmer's bank account, and ability to participate in inter-mandi and inter-state trade. Required documents for first-time registration: Aadhaar, mobile number and bank passbook. Registration is free at any e-NAM mandi or through the e-NAM mobile app. As of 2025 over 1,400 mandis across 28 states/UTs are integrated.

entries.topic: Drought-tolerant practices — moisture conservation and crop choice
entries.content: In rainfed and drought-prone areas, the proven moisture-conservation package is: (1) summer ploughing to a depth of 15-20 cm to break the hard pan; (2) creation of compartmental bunds or contour bunding to slow runoff; (3) tied-ridging across the slope for row crops; (4) mulching with crop residues, polythene or weed biomass to cut evaporation losses by 30-40%; (5) zai pits and trench planting for high-value horticultural crops. Drought-tolerant crop and variety choices: pearl millet (HHB-67 Improved, HHB-299), sorghum (CSV-15, CSV-22), finger millet (GPU-28, GPU-66), pigeon pea (BSMR-736), groundnut (TG-37A) and short-duration pulses. Always pair drought-tolerant varieties with seed treatment, balanced nutrition and timely sowing — variety alone is not enough.

entries.topic: FPO formation under 10,000 FPO scheme — eligibility and benefits
entries.content: The Government's scheme for formation and promotion of 10,000 Farmer Producer Organisations (FPOs) provides ₹18 lakh per FPO over a five-year handholding period through implementing agencies like NABARD, SFAC and NCDC. Each FPO is professionally managed by a Cluster Based Business Organisation (CBBO) for the handholding period and receives equity grant up to ₹2,000 per farmer (max ₹15 lakh per FPO) and credit guarantee cover up to ₹2 crore. Eligibility: minimum 300 farmer-members in plains and 100 in north-eastern / hilly areas, registered as a Producer Company under the Companies Act 2013 or as a Cooperative Society. FPOs get easier access to KCC, post-harvest infrastructure, custom hiring centres and e-NAM trading.

entries.topic: Maharashtra — Namo Shetkari Maha Samman Nidhi (state add-on)
entries.content: STATE OVERLAY for Maharashtra (EXAMPLE entry — verify the current amount and eligibility with the Maharashtra Agriculture Department before relying on it). Namo Shetkari Maha Samman Nidhi is a Maharashtra state scheme that pays eligible farmer families an annual amount IN ADDITION TO the central PM-KISAN benefit, credited in instalments to the same registered PM-KISAN beneficiaries. Enrol / check status through the Maharashtra agriculture portal or the nearest taluka agriculture office. NOTE: the exact yearly amount and instalment schedule change by state notification — always confirm the latest figure before advising.

entries.topic: Telangana — Rythu Bandhu / Rythu Bharosa (state add-on)
entries.content: STATE OVERLAY for Telangana (EXAMPLE entry — verify the current per-acre amount, season coverage and eligibility with the Telangana Agriculture Department before relying on it). Rythu Bandhu (now Rythu Bharosa) is a Telangana state investment-support scheme that pays land-owning farmers a per-acre amount each season to help with input costs, separate from central schemes. Check enrolment and the current per-acre rate through the state Rythu Bharosa portal or the local agriculture / revenue office. NOTE: the per-acre amount, season coverage and eligibility change by state notification — always confirm the latest figure before advising.
